name: Omani Arabic

allophone_rules.notes: Velar affrication /k/ → [tʃ] adjacent to a high front vowel /i, iː/. Al-Balushi 2016 (Macrolinguistics 4(1):80-125), p.89: 'while this affrication (from /k/ to /č/) occurs in the Bedouin dialects in the vicinity of front vowels, it is unconditional in the sedentary dialects (Holes, 2013)' — with examples bāčir 'tomorrow' (bākir), simač 'fish' (samak), čiswah 'bride's clothes' (kiswah) from the Al-Buraimi, Ṣūr and Jaʕlān Bedouin dialects. Note that 'sedentary' is not uniform in Al-Balushi's account: the northern sedentary varieties of Wādi Saħtan and Al-Ħamrā DO affricate (yčūn, ʔačil, čēčah), and there the process is 'unconditional' (not restricted to the front-vowel environment), whereas the prestige sedentary koine of Muscat and Ṣalālah does NOT productively affricate /k/, and Dhofari does not. LIMITATIONS: this rule models the front-vowel-conditioned Bedouin/interior pattern only; it therefore over-generalises toward the affricating varieties and cannot capture either the non-affricating Muscat/Ṣalālah norm or the coda / unconditional-northern-sedentary distributions. UNAFFECTED by this rule: Old Arabic *q, which in Omani is retained as [q] (sedentary; grapheme ق lists [q] first), unlike the mainstream Gulf *q→[ɡ].

ancestors.notes: Peninsular Arabic base — conservative consonant system, interdental retention, emphatic spreading (AR_PEN_EMPH_*).
Persian adstrate from Hormuz Strait trade and Muscat contacts.

notes: Omani Arabic — a conservative Peninsular variety of the southeastern coast, genetically Peninsular (inherits ar-x-peninsular DIRECTLY, not the Gulf node). DISTINCTIVE from the northern Gulf: Old Arabic *q is RETAINED as [q] in the sedentary varieties of Muscat, Ṣalālah and parts of the Bāṭina (Al-Balushi 2016 p.88: '...pronounce /q/ as /q/, as in ħaqqūti "my/mine"...'), while Bedouin varieties have *q → [ɡ] (y-ɡūl 'he says') and some northern-mountain sedentary varieties have *q → [k]; Sur/Ṣoħār/Ṣaħam show *q → [dʒ] (Holes 2014). The grapheme ق therefore lists [q] first (sedentary default), [ɡ] second. jiim /dʒ/ is realised as [dʒ] in the (mainly Bedouin/northern) dialects that keep it and as [ɡ] elsewhere (Glover 1988:38, cited Al-Balushi p.89: 'the voiced palatal affricate /ǰ/ is a free variant of /g/ in most words'). Interdentals /θ ð ðˤ/ and emphatics are retained (Al-Balushi p.87). /k/→[tʃ] affrication is present as a Bedouin/interior feature (allophone_rule OM_K_AFFRICATION; see its notes for the sedentary caveat). South Arabian (Mehri/Jibbali) adstrate in Dhofar; Swahili adstrate from the Omani-Zanzibar maritime empire; Persian adstrate from Hormuz trade. Input contract: fully diacritised (tashkeel) Arabic.

sources.author: Al-Balushi, R.
sources.title: Omani Arabic: More than a Dialect
sources.notes: Read the phonology sections (pp. 85-90, open-access PDF). Primary source for Omani /q/ reflexes (Bedouin [ɡ]; sedentary Muscat/Ṣalālah [q]; northern-mountain [k]; Sur/Ṣoħār [dʒ]) at p.88; jiim /dʒ/~[ɡ] free variation (Glover 1988:38) at p.89; /k/→[tʃ] Bedouin 'in the vicinity of front vowels' vs unconditional sedentary (Holes 2013) at p.89; interdental/emphatic retention at p.87; emphatic backing /a/→[ɑ] (marað̣ [mɑṛɑðˤ]) at p.90.

sources.author: Alshammari, K. K.
sources.title: An Optimality-Theoretic Account of Velar Affrication in Northern Najdi Arabic
sources.publisher: Journal of Language Teaching and Research 17(4)
sources.notes: Read in full (open-access PDF). Cross-reference for the Peninsular velar-affrication typology (front-vowel conditioning; historical/lexicalised status per Johnstone 1967) underlying OM_K_AFFRICATION.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Omani Arabic.

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, §2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVːC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiˈtaːb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVː/CVC) does (muˈdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ˈmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant — a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
